name: Relay Financial SMB Banking API
what_it_does: Relay Financial SMB banking REST API for small businesses and accounting firms to manage multi-account business banking, automate expense categorization, and integrate with accounting software for bookkeeping-friendly financial management. Enables AI agents to manage multiple business checking accounts (up to 20) for expense categorization and budget management automation, handle business debit card issuance and spend controls for employee expense management automation, access ACH and wire transfer processing for business payment automation, retrieve real-time transaction feeds with accounting categorization for bookkeeping automation, manage accounting firm access and client banking visibility for practice management automation, handle automated savings and reserve fund segregation for cash management automation, access Relay Bill Pay for supplier payment automation, retrieve CSV and accounting software export for bookkeeping workflow automation, manage team member access and permissions for business banking governance automation, and integrate Relay with QuickBooks Online, Xero, FreshBooks, and Wave for end-to-end SMB banking-to-bookkeeping automation.
use_cases: Managing multi-account budgets from SMB cash allocation agents
Issuing team debit cards from employee spend management agents
Syncing transactions from bookkeeping automation agents
Providing client banking visibility from accounting firm agents
not_for: Enterprise treasury management with complex banking needs
Merchant payment acquiring and POS (use Stripe or Square)
best_when: A small business or accounting firm wanting AI agents to automate multi-account budget management, team card issuance, transaction categorization, and accounting software synchronization through Relay's SMB banking platform.
avoid_when: BANKING PARTNER DEPENDENCY ON THREAD BANK: Relay accounts are held at Thread Bank (FDIC member); Relay is a neobank front-end, not a bank; automated cash management must understand Relay's banking partner structure; Thread Bank operational issues affect Relay account availability. MULTI-ACCOUNT STRUCTURE PLANNING FOR AUTOMATED ALLOCATION: Relay's strength is multi-account structure (up to 20 checking accounts); effective automated cash allocation requires upfront account architecture planning; automated cash allocation deployed on a single account misses Relay's core multi-account value proposition. ACCOUNTING SYNC CATEGORY MAPPING MAINTENANCE: Relay accounting sync to QuickBooks and Xero maps Relay transaction categories to accounting categories; automated bookkeeping depends on category mapping being current; new vendor or expense types may create uncategorized transactions requiring mapping maintenance; implement category mapping review in automated bookkeeping workflow.

auth.notes: Relay uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. REST API with JSON. Toronto, Canada HQ (operates in US). Founded 2018 by Yoseph West and Omid Ashtari. Backed by Bain Capital Ventures, FJ Labs, Garage Capital ($44M+ raised). Banking partner: Thread Bank (formerly known as First Internet Bank or similar — FDIC member). Products: Business checking (up to 20 accounts), business debit cards (up to 50), ACH, wires, Bill Pay. Accounting firm partner program. Integration: QuickBooks Online, Xero, FreshBooks, Wave. Profit First methodology alignment (multi-account cash management). Competes with Mercury, Novo, and Found for SMB neobanking.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free Relay Standard with basic features; Relay Pro at $30/month for advanced features
pricing.paid_tiers: Free (Relay Standard); Relay Pro $30/month (automated savings, team spend controls, priority support)
pricing.notes: Toronto Canada / US operations. Bain Capital Ventures backed. Free Standard + $30/mo Pro. FDIC via Thread Bank.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; account and transaction queries throttled; webhooks for transaction, transfer, and card events

known_agent_gotchas: MULTI-ACCOUNT TRANSFER INTERNAL vs EXTERNAL PROCESSING: Relay internal transfers between Relay accounts process instantly; external ACH transfers from Relay to other banks process on standard ACH timeline (1-3 business days); automated cash allocation workflows must distinguish internal (instant) vs external (ACH-timed) transfers for liquidity planning

DEBIT CARD DAILY SPEND LIMIT FOR AUTOMATED PURCHASES: Relay debit cards have daily spend limits; automated procurement using Relay debit cards must verify available daily limit before purchase; automated purchasing that exceeds daily limit creates declined transaction without pre-authorization check

ACCOUNTING SYNC TIMING FOR MULTI-ACCOUNT RECONCILIATION: Relay accounting sync exports transactions from all accounts but reconciliation mapping must correctly attribute transactions to accounting categories by account; automated bookkeeping for multi-account Relay structure must maintain account-to-accounting-category mapping; single-account bookkeeping automation misapplied to multi-account structure creates category mixing

BILL PAY PROCESSING TIME FOR SUPPLIER PAYMENTS: Relay Bill Pay processes payments by check (7-10 business days) or ACH (3-5 business days); automated Bill Pay scheduling must account for processing time relative to supplier payment terms; automated payment scheduled on due date creates late payment for non-instant payment methods

ACCOUNTING FIRM ACCESS PERMISSION GRANULARITY: Relay accounting firm access provides read-only view of client banking; automated bookkeeping firm workflows using Relay access cannot initiate transactions on behalf of client; automated AP payment workflows requiring transaction initiation must use client-authorized access, not accounting firm read-only access
agent_readiness.security_notes: SMB multi-account banking. FDIC pass-through, FinCEN, BSA. OAuth2. US. Business account and transaction data.

security_score_components.security_notes: SMB banking. FDIC pass-through, FinCEN, BSA. OAuth2. US. Business account and transaction data.
